danger_signals.visual_form: Lava pools, fire traps, large drops rendered in landscape
danger_signals.meaning: Instant death zones; lemmings drown, burn, or fall fatally

danger_signals.visual_form: Tall cliffs and elevated terrain edges
danger_signals.meaning: Falling from excessive height causes lemming death
Expiration causes all lemmings to explode regardless of state

danger_signals.visual_form: Bottom boundary of visible play area

safety_signals.visual_form: Designated exit location marked on terrain
safety_signals.meaning: Target destination; reaching this saves lemmings
Safe walkable surface; lemmings traverse safely

safety_signals.visual_form: Empty space where Builder skill creates stairways

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Entrance trap doors release steady stream from above

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Clear, unobstructed terrain path rendered with accessible platforms
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemmings walk forward in line, following predecessors

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Terrain blocks, walls, drops, or hazards rendered as solid geometry
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemmings reverse direction upon contact (unless assigned skill), attempt to traverse around obstacle

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hazard contact (lava, fire, trap, excessive fall) shown visually
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemming sprite disappears; death count incremented

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Timer visibly counting down on UI; visual distress may escalate
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: If timer expires, all lemmings detonate regardless of location/status

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Lemming sprite or animation changes to reflect skill state (climber stance, bomber timer, parachute deployment, etc.)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Lemming behavior alters: climbs walls, digs terrain, builds stairs, blocks others, explodes, etc.

environmental_storytelling.technique: Hazard placement as level design language
environmental_storytelling.example: Lava pools, fire traps, and drops positioned to require specific skill combinations; visual layout communicates puzzle intent
Early levels use simple obstacles (isolated drops); later levels layer multiple hazards (bridges over lava, narrow pathways with traps) to increase visual/mechanical challenge

environmental_storytelling.technique: Landscape geometry as skill requirement signpost
environmental_storytelling.example: Vertical cliffs visually suggest Climber skill needed; horizontal barriers suggest Basher; downward slopes suggest Digger use

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Follows other lemmings in line, ignores collisions (except Blockers), turns around when hitting impassable obstacle, falls off edges
Scales vertical surfaces; falls if encountering overhang
Deploys parachute; descends safely from any height
Explodes after 5-second timer; destroys destructible landscape in proximity; does not harm other lemmings or traps
Stands still; reverses direction of any lemming hitting it
Constructs 12-step stairway; stops on collision with head, depletion of bricks, or solid object
Basher tunnels horizontally; Miner diagonally downward; Digger directly downward; all cannot breach steel barriers

notes: Lemmings uses non-violent visual communication—danger is signaled through environmental hazards and physics, not faction conflict. The game's world-state manifestation is entirely mechanical: visuals show terrain, hazards, and skill effects; behavior emerges from lemming AI and player-assigned abilities. No political or faction systems present; the game is a pure resource-management puzzle where lemmings are guided entities, not autonomous agents with agency. Visual design relies on immediate environmental legibility—hazards, safe zones, and skill affordances must be instantly recognizable for effective puzzle-solving.
